paper_title: Arid Land Vegetation Analysis and Estimation of the Potential Ancient Arable Range

methods.description: This method involves using PALSAR-1 data to extract arid land vegetation as indicators of potential ancient arable land. The analysis considers the state and distribution of psammophytes and phreatophytes in relation to groundwater levels.

steps.description: PALSAR-1 was applied to extract the arid land vegetation that serves as an indicator of potential ancient arable land.
steps.searchable_summary: use PALSAR-1 data to extract arid land vegetation

steps.description: The state and distribution of psammophytes, such as red willow, and phreatophytes, such as reeds, change as the groundwater level changes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze vegetation distribution in relation to groundwater levels

steps.description: In terms of archaeological discovery, targets such as ditches and channels are easier to detect if they are covered by less sand.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze archaeological targets based on vegetation cover

methods.description: This method uses X- and L-band SAR data to reconstruct the paleoenvironment of water cultivation dating back to the Han and Tang Dynasties around the Yangguan Frontier Pass.

steps.description: The spaceborne SAR data, i.e., two frames of X-band TerraSAR-X/TanDEM-X (TSX/TDX) data and six frames of L-band PALSAR-1 archived data, were acquired.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire X- and L-band SAR data

steps.description: Using these time-series data, we can determine changes due to the moisture and permittivity anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: process SAR data to identify moisture and permittivity anomalies
